_comment: Cisco Umbrella DNS log field inventory, scored against OCSF DNS Activity (4003). Umbrella DNS logs are gzip CSV written every 10 minutes to a managed/self-managed S3 bucket; this inventory is the documented v3+ DNS-log column set (13 columns), corroborated by a real sample line in the Cyderes parser mirror. Newer schema versions add identity-UUID and DNS-over-HTTPS columns and the proxy-log Request Method; those are out of scope here and noted in PROVENANCE. Proxy/IP/cloud-firewall logs are separate Umbrella log types scored to other OCSF classes. Field names are Umbrella's documented column headers. Transcribed from the Cisco Umbrella 'Log Formats and Versioning' / 'DNS Log Formats' docs; corroborated against the Cyderes and Google SecOps (Chronicle) Umbrella-DNS parsers. See PROVENANCE.md.
source_schema: Cisco Umbrella DNS log (CSV, v3+ column set)

fields.note: time the DNS request was made (UTC)
the most specific identity that made the request (heterogeneous: a roaming computer, AD user, network, site, ...)
comma-list of ALL identities associated with the request (device + user + network + AD groups)
internal client IP that made the request
egress / NAT public IP the request came from
DNS response code: NOERROR / NXDOMAIN / SERVFAIL / ...
Umbrella content/security categories the domain matched (comma-list, Umbrella taxonomy)
type of the most granular identity: Roaming Computer / AD User / AD Computer / Network / Site / Mobile Device / ...
comma-list of the types of all associated identities
the categories that triggered the block (Umbrella taxonomy)
